findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Bold emphasis completely removed from the consumer advisory body text on page 1. The PDF renders the entire advisory warning in bold (poster format), but the HTML body text is in regular weight.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1: All body text ('Eating raw, or undercooked oysters, clams or mussels may cause severe illness...') is rendered in bold. HTML: The same body text is rendered in regular (non-bold) weight. 'oysters, clams or mussels' retains italic but loses bold.

findings.description: Bold emphasis completely removed from the legal failure-to-post warning on page 2. The PDF renders this paragraph in bold to highlight its legal significance, but the HTML renders it in regular weight.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: 'Failure to post this Consumer Advisory is a violation of the "Rules Governing the Sanitation of Restaurants and Other Foodhandling Establishments"...' is entirely bold. HTML: The same paragraph is rendered in regular (non-bold) weight.
